identifiers: extra-wide or standard slots per model

symptom: Uneven browning, wrong toast shade, or English muffin issues (Hamilton Beach tips)

preconditions: Toaster plugged in only during active toasting
Do not operate unattended; unplug after use
hazards: Fire if unattended or wrong foods used (no toaster pastries in slot — use toaster oven per manual)

steps.action: Remember: bread lifter will not latch down unless the toaster is plugged in
steps.what_to_look_for: User expects lever to stay down — if unplugged, lever will not latch
steps.visual_confirmation: Plug connected if user wants to toast

steps.action: Adjust shade for bread temperature: room-temperature bread often toasts medium on settings 2–4; refrigerated bread may need a darker setting
steps.what_to_look_for: User describes toast too light — ask if bread was cold from fridge
steps.visual_confirmation: Shade dial moved appropriately after explanation

steps.action: For English muffins: irregular surface may need more than one cycle; after one cycle on highest, lower shade for second cycle if darker needed — watch to avoid overbrowning
steps.what_to_look_for: Muffin uneven or underdone on first cycle
steps.visual_confirmation: Second short cycle with user watching browning
steps.if_issue_found: English muffins cut with a knife toast more evenly than those split with a fork (manual)

steps.action: When toasting a single slice, bread may be placed in any slot; expect possible side-to-side color variation — this is normal
steps.what_to_look_for: User has one slice and worries about uneven color
steps.visual_confirmation: User accepts variation or rotates slice for second pass if needed

steps.action: Use STOP button to stop a cycle at any time, then unplug when done
steps.visual_confirmation: Toasting stops; unit unplugged after use

sources: Hamilton Beach Use and Care Guide 840231800 — Tips and Troubleshooting / Toasting Chart
